title: Closed resources should not be accessed
description: Closed resources should not be accessedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugsymbolic-executioncertUsing the value of a pointer to aFILEobject after the associated file is closed is undefined behavior.Why is this an issue?What is the potential impact?How can I fix it?More InfoOnce a file has been closed, its correspondingFILE*typed variable becomes invalid and the stream may no longer be accessed through this variable. In particular, a pointer to aFILEobject may not be passed tofclosemore than once.Using the value of a pointer to aFILEobject after the associated file is closed results in undefined behavior.#include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> int process_file(int print) { FILE *f = fopen("example.txt", "r"); if (!f) { perror("fopen() failed"); return 1; } if (print) { char
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
